title: What are semantic HTML elements?
content: Explain semantic HTML elements like header, nav, main, section, article, and footer. Why are they important?

title: Difference between block and inline elements
content: What's the difference between block-level and inline elements in HTML? Provide examples of each.

title: What are HTML forms?
content: Explain HTML forms and different input types. How do you validate form data?

title: What is the DOCTYPE declaration?
content: What is the DOCTYPE declaration in HTML? Why is it important and what happens if you omit it?

title: HTML5 new features
content: What are the new features introduced in HTML5? Discuss canvas, video, audio, and local storage.

title: What is HTML?
content: HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the standard language used to create and structure content on the web.

title: What are HTML tags?
content: Tags are keywords enclosed in angle brackets that define the structure and elements of a web page, such as p, h1, div.

title: Difference between div and span
content: div is a block-level element used for grouping content, while span is an inline element used for styling small text portions.

title: HTML code: hyperlink to example.com
content: Add a clickable link with text 'Visit Example' that redirects to https://example.com.

title: HTML code: insert an image
content: Insert an image named 'photo.jpg' with alternative text 'My Photo'.

title: Purpose of DOCTYPE html
content: It declares the document type and version of HTML to the browser, ensuring correct rendering.

title: HTML code: ordered and unordered list
content: Create one ordered list with numbers and one unordered list with bullets containing at least 3 items each.

title: Absolute vs relative URLs in HTML
content: Absolute URLs specify the full path (http://example.com/page), while relative URLs specify location relative to the current page.

title: HTML code: table with 3 rows and 3 columns
content: Design a table containing student names and marks in 3 subjects.

title: Difference between id and class attributes
content: id is unique and identifies a single element, while class can be used for multiple elements for styling or scripting.

title: HTML code: login form
content: Create a login form containing username, password fields, and a submit button.

title: Use of meta tag in HTML
content: meta provides metadata about the HTML document like character encoding, author, viewport settings, and SEO keywords.

title: HTML code: embed a video
content: Embed a video file named 'movie.mp4' that has controls to play/pause.

title: HTML code: navigation links
content: Create a navigation bar with links: Home, About, Contact.

title: What are HTML attributes?
content: Attributes provide additional information about elements, e.g., href in a, src in img, style in p.

title: HTML code: add an audio file
content: Embed an audio file named 'song.mp3' with controls enabled.

title: b/strong vs i/em
content: b and i are for styling bold/italic text, while strong and em indicate importance or emphasis semantically.
